expected_diagnosis: Bearing degradation stage 2 (inner race defect). Vibration RMS exceeds ISO 10816-3 Zone C threshold. Elevated acoustic emission confirms metal-to-metal contact. Oil pressure drop suggests lubrication film breakdown.
maintenance_action: Schedule bearing replacement within 72 hours. Reduce spindle speed to 1200 RPM interim. Increase lubrication frequency to every 4 hours. Order SKF 6210-2RS replacement bearings.

expected_diagnosis: Normal operating condition. All parameters within acceptable range per OEM specifications. Vibration in ISO 10816-3 Zone A. Temperature consistent with rated load.
maintenance_action: Continue standard preventive maintenance schedule. Next inspection due per PM calendar in 30 days. Log readings in CMMS for trend analysis.

expected_diagnosis: Motor overload condition with insulation degradation. Current draw 147% of rated capacity indicates mechanical binding or misalignment. Winding temperature exceeds Class F insulation rating. Insulation resistance critically low (minimum 5 MOhm per IEEE 43).
maintenance_action: Immediate shutdown required. Perform megger test to confirm insulation condition. Inspect conveyor for mechanical binding, foreign object obstruction, or belt misalignment. Motor likely requires rewinding or replacement. Check thermal overload relay calibration.

expected_diagnosis: Reduced compressor efficiency (76% of rated capacity). Elevated discharge temperature suggests worn rotary element or insufficient cooling. Pressure below setpoint with reduced flow indicates possible air-end seal leakage. Elevated dew point suggests dryer malfunction.
maintenance_action: Schedule air-end inspection within 2 weeks. Check coolant level and radiator for blockage. Test inlet valve operation. Inspect coalescing filters in refrigerated dryer. Conduct air leak audit on downstream distribution system.

expected_diagnosis: Dissolved gas analysis indicates thermal fault (T2 classification per IEEE C57.104). Hydrogen and methane levels exceed caution thresholds. Acetylene presence at 3.5 ppm suggests low-energy arcing. DGA trending shows 15% monthly increase. Duval Triangle analysis points to thermal fault 300-700C range.
maintenance_action: Increase DGA sampling to weekly. Schedule infrared thermography of bushings and tap changer. Plan oil processing and degassing within 30 days. Prepare contingency for load transfer to backup transformer if acetylene exceeds 7 ppm.

expected_diagnosis: Tool wear approaching end-of-life. Flank wear VB at 0.28mm exceeds recommended change threshold of 0.25mm for carbide inserts on hardened steel. Surface roughness degrading from baseline 1.6 Ra. Elevated spindle load confirms increased cutting forces from worn edge geometry.
maintenance_action: Replace cutting inserts at next tool change interval or current part completion. Verify tool offset compensation. Inspect toolholder for runout exceeding 0.005mm. Adjust feed rate to 180 mm/min if production cannot pause for immediate change.

expected_diagnosis: Severe feedwater chemistry deviation. pH critically low (target 8.5-9.5) indicates chemical feed system failure. Elevated iron confirms active corrosion in condensate return piping. Dissolved oxygen above 0.007 ppm limit indicates deaerator malfunction or air in-leakage.
maintenance_action: Immediately verify chemical injection pumps and sulfite feed system. Inspect deaerator spray valves and vent condenser. Perform condensate system leak check. Blow down boiler to reduce dissolved solids. Do not operate above 50% load until chemistry is corrected.

expected_diagnosis: Shaft misalignment — combined parallel and angular. 1X vibration dominant in radial direction with significant 2X component confirms angular misalignment. Offset of 0.42mm exceeds 0.05mm tolerance for flexible coupling at 3600 RPM. Coupling temperature elevated from flex element fatigue.
maintenance_action: Schedule laser alignment correction within 1 week. Inspect coupling flex elements for cracking or deformation. Check mounting bolts for looseness. Verify soft foot condition on motor base. After alignment, recheck vibration to confirm correction below 2.5 mm/s.

expected_diagnosis: Hydraulic system contamination with servo valve degradation. ISO cleanliness code 22/20/17 far exceeds target 16/14/11 for servo-proportional systems. Extended cycle time (150% of rated) indicates restricted flow from contaminated servo valve. Position accuracy still within tolerance but degrading.
maintenance_action: Install kidney-loop filtration system with 3-micron absolute elements. Schedule servo valve cleaning or replacement. Flush hydraulic lines. Replace return-line filter elements. Re-sample oil after 24 hours of filtration to verify cleanliness improvement.

expected_diagnosis: Combustion inefficiency with incomplete combustion. CO at 320 ppm indicates insufficient air-fuel mixing or burner fouling. NOx elevated suggesting hot spots from flame impingement. O2 at 4.8% is above optimal 2-3% range. Flame scanner signal degraded from baseline 0.90, indicating dirty lens or flame instability.
maintenance_action: Schedule burner tuning and combustion optimization. Clean flame scanner lenses. Inspect burner diffuser and gas spuds for fouling. Verify air damper linkage and actuator calibration. Perform O2 trim controller calibration. Target CO below 50 ppm and O2 at 2.5% post-tune.
